title: Dragon Appears in the Storm
description: On pages 10–13, dragon appears in the storm focuses on Luffy, Zoro, Nami, Usopp as a storm, Smoker, and a mysterious intervention push the Straw Hats out of Loguetown and toward the Grand Line.
scene: On pages 10–13, dragon appears in the storm focuses on Luffy, Zoro, Nami, Usopp as a storm, Smoker, and a mysterious intervention push the Straw Hats out of Loguetown and toward the Grand Line.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Goddann it! Why that flashy rubber brat with the luck of a devil...!

dialogue.speaker: Buggy
dialogue.text: Capt. Buggy! You survived the lightning!?

dialogue.speaker: Alvida
dialogue.text: These Marines are from the head- quarters!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: We'll be in a tight spot if some big- shot marine shows up!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Hmph, as if pathetic shot-nosed marines could ever...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Who just said my nose is shot- nosed!?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: It ain't over yet, boys! That rubber brat won't be able to leave this island!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: He and his crew probably have no clue that their ship has been burnt to ashes by now!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: So let's finish'em off and haul ass outta here!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I don't have the time to deal with you runts.

dialogue.speaker: Smoker
dialogue.text: S-SO strong! So that's the moku moku devil fruit's ability...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: We've all been rounded up, Cap'n!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: He caught buggy's entire crew in an instant...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: What's with this weird iron net that they shot out from their guns?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Since most of the big-name crooks are devil fruit users, the Marines have specially developed these "imprisoning rounds" to deal with them!

summary: This part closes East Blue with a storm-driven escape and the crew’s first real step toward the Grand Line.
